# fmc_network_overrides (Resource)

This resource manages a Network Overrides.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "fmc_network_overrides" "example" {
  parent_name = "my_network"
  parent_id   = "12345678-90ab-cdef-1234-567890abcdef"
  overrides = [
    {
      target_id   = "12345678-90ab-cdef-1234-567890abcdef"
      target_type = "Device"
      description = "My Network object"
      prefix      = "10.1.2.0/24"
    }
  ]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `overrides` (Attributes List) Override entries for the object. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--overrides))
- `parent_id` (String) ID of the parent Network object.
- `parent_name` (String) Name of the parent Network object.

### Optional

- `domain` (String) Name of the FMC domain

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Id of the object

<a id="nestedatt--overrides"></a>
### Nested Schema for `overrides`

Required:

- `prefix` (String) Prefix of the Network.
- `target_id` (String) ID of the override target Device or Domain. Note that each target can be defined once only.
- `target_type` (String) Type of the target.
  - Choices: `Device`, `Domain`

Optional:

- `description` (String) Description of the overridden object.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# <domain> is optional. If not provided, `Global` is used implicitly and resource's `domain` attribute is not set.
terraform import fmc_network_overrides.example "<domain>,<id>"
```
